stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 (April 21, 2026): PAT Rs 660 Cr (+19% YoY, ~14% beat); FY26 PAT Rs 2,291 Cr (+18% YoY). Q4 retail disbursements Rs 9,020 Cr (+32% YoY); AUM Rs 90,921 Cr (+13%); retail loan book Rs 86,946 Cr (+16%). NIM 3.69% (+6 bps QoQ); GNPA 0.93% (sub-1% milestone); Capital Adequacy 27.26%. Final dividend Rs 8/share recommended. Stock at Rs 1,065.60 on May 6 (+2.23% intraday, ~+30% trailing month). MOFSL 'Buy'; ICICI Securities upgraded to 'BUY'. Promoter PNB ~28% stake.
stocks.sector_context: ICRA expects HFC on-book portfolio +13-15% in FY26 (sector book ~Rs 8.8 lakh Cr). Housing finance NBFCs benefit from stable rate environment, improving asset quality and rising affordable housing demand. Sector valuation grade has rotated from 'attractive' to 'fair' as HFC stocks have rallied off lows.
stocks.peer_comparison: GNPA 0.93% now competitive with best-in-class HFC peers (Can Fin Homes ~0.43% net NPA). Retail growth +16% outpaces sector ~14%. Q4 disbursement growth +32% YoY meaningfully above peer set. Trades at premium to LICHF (P/E ~5.8x) but justified by growth and asset-quality trajectory.
red_flags: Stock +30% in past month — momentum extended after Q4 results
Sector valuation grade rotated from attractive to fair — re-rating largely played
Provision write-back contributed to Q4 beat — recurring earnings quality less clear
PNB promoter stake ~28% — any future dilution/exit could overhang
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 8/share — pending shareholder approval at AGM
Affordable & Emerging Markets segment 18-20% growth target
Continued GNPA improvement below 1%
Possible rating action post 27.26% capital adequacy

stocks.recent_news: ETF tracks domestic physical gold; trading Rs 124.39 on May 7 (+0.30%). 52-week range Rs 76.36-148.14; AUM Rs 54,127.54 Cr; expense ratio 0.80% (Nippon India MF). MCX 24K gold June expiry at Rs 1,52,950/10g on May 8 (+0.45%) — second day of gains driven by Iran war uncertainty and ahead of US NFP/unemployment data. Futures still ~15% below Rs 1,80,779 lifetime high (Jan 29, 2026).
stocks.sector_context: Gold remains in structural bull regime — global spot gold supported by central-bank buying, geopolitical tensions and Fed rate-cut expectations. INR weakness adds rupee-denominated tailwind. US-Iran tensions and Middle East de-escalation hopes drive intraday volatility around safe-haven flows. Pending NFP data is the near-term Fed-policy swing factor.
stocks.peer_comparison: Largest Indian gold ETF by AUM (Rs 54,127.54 Cr) — preferred liquidity vehicle vs Kotak Gold ETF, HDFC Gold ETF, ICICI Pru Gold ETF. 0.80% expense ratio is in line with peer set. Tracks domestic gold spot with tight tracking error.
red_flags: ~15-16% off 52-week and lifetime high (Rs 148.14 / underlying Rs 1,80,779) — suggests consolidation, not fresh breakout
US-Iran de-escalation could compress safe-haven premium near-term
Strong NFP print could revive USD and pressure gold
0.80% expense ratio drag vs holding physical/sovereign gold bonds
catalysts: US NFP and unemployment data — potential Fed dovish trigger
Continued Middle East geopolitical risk
Central-bank gold accumulation
INR weakness amplifying rupee gold returns

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 (May 5, 2026): Consolidated PAT Rs 408 Cr (+18.26% YoY); revenue Rs 3,333 Cr (+22% YoY). EBITDA margin 15.63% vs 16.78% YoY — copra inflation cited. FY26 revenue Rs 13,611 Cr (+16%); FY26 PAT Rs 1,813 Cr (vs Rs 1,658 Cr). Final dividend Rs 4/share; record date July 30, 2026; payment by September 5; 38th AGM August 6, 2026. Stock hit record Rs 843.15 on May 6 (+4.5%); trading Rs 832.15 on May 7. MOFSL 'Buy' TP Rs 950 (~15% upside).
stocks.sector_context: Nifty FMCG outperforming Nifty50 with sector rotating to volume growth. Easing input costs (especially copra, down 35-40% from peak) supportive of margin recovery in H2 FY26/FY27. Crude up ~70% YoY in April 2026 has reintroduced selective margin pressure across packaging-heavy peers. Britannia, Nestle, Marico flagged as least impacted by Q1 raw-material inflation.
stocks.peer_comparison: Marico Q4 revenue +22% materially outpaces HUL (~+6% volume) and Dabur (geopolitical headwinds in international). Nestle and HUL face analyst downgrades on cost inflation while Marico benefits from copra deflation. Premium-rated but justified by growth — Motilal Oswal calls Marico a 'top consumer pick'.
red_flags: EBITDA margin contracted ~115 bps YoY on copra inflation in Q4 (lagging effect)
Stock at all-time high — momentum extended
Bangladesh and MENA international exposure carries geopolitical risk
Monsoon dependence for rural FMCG demand
catalysts: Copra prices down 35-40% from peak — management guides 150-200 bps EBITDA margin expansion in FY27
Final dividend Rs 4/share, record date July 30, 2026
38th AGM August 6, 2026
MOFSL TP Rs 950 implies ~15% upside
Premiumization in foods and digital-first brands

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 (announced May 5-6, 2026): Net profit Rs 612.3 Cr (vs Rs 261.2 Cr YoY) — boosted by one-time tax benefit and operational strength. Q4 revenue Rs 4,450.4 Cr (+30% YoY INR; +21.2% YoY USD at $489.1 mn). EBITDA Rs 916.8 Cr (margin 20.6%); EBIT margin 16.6% — record-high. FY26 revenue Rs 16,420.7 Cr; FY26 PAT Rs 1,555.7 Cr. Encora acquisition closed at $2.5B EV in April 2026 (Q4 absorbed Rs 50.1 Cr integration cost). Order executable $1.75 bn. FY27 EBITDA margin guidance >20.5%. Stock +10% to Rs 1,294.15 high on May 6; Rs 1,283.80 on May 7 (+0.24%). PL Capital 'Buy' TP Rs 2,020 (~57% upside). May 8 news: Coforge-Cigniti merger details disclosed.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty IT showing fresh strength on AI-demand expectations and global tech beta. Mid-cap IT regaining earnings momentum after soft FY25; Coforge's Q4 reinforces sector AI thesis. US discretionary spend recovering selectively in BFSI and Travel verticals. Encora deal positions Coforge as a $2.5 bn AI/engineering services player.
stocks.peer_comparison: Encora deal narrows scale gap to Mphasis/LTIMindtree but Coforge still well below TCS/Infosys/Wipro. FY27 margin guidance >20.5% positions it at sector-leading mid-cap profitability. Brokerage targets dispersed: PL TP Rs 2,020 (Buy); Macquarie raised TP +77%; Elara cautious with 'Accumulate' TP Rs 1,380 — flagging EV/sales 3.9x as expensive given Encora's 7-10% organic growth.
red_flags: Q4 PAT optically inflated by one-time tax benefit — recurring quality less clear
Elara Capital cautious with 'Accumulate' TP Rs 1,380 — below current price
Encora EV/sales 3.9x flagged as expensive given Encora's 7-10% organic growth
Large $2.5 bn deal integration risk relative to Coforge's pre-deal scale
Cigniti merger adds another integration workstream
Stock at multi-month high after +10% pop — momentum extended
catalysts: Encora deal close — combined $2.5 bn entity; FY27 ramp
FY27 EBITDA margin guidance >20.5%
AI/engineering revenue acceleration
PL TP Rs 2,020, Macquarie raised TP +77%
BFSI and Travel vertical momentum

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 (announced April 29, 2026): Net profit Rs 303.1 Cr (+24% YoY); revenue Rs 2,119.2 Cr (+29% YoY); EBITDA growth +60.8% YoY; EBITDA margin 16.8%. FY26 PAT Rs 747.9 Cr (vs Rs 527.4 Cr); FY26 revenue Rs 7,002 Cr (vs Rs 5,075 Cr). Final dividend Rs 6.70/share. Stock gapped +14% on results day (Apr 29) opening at Rs 3,100; trading Rs 2,871.70 on May 5; +48% in April 2026. MoD nominated Dinesh Mahur as Government Nominee Director effective April 27, 2026 (replacing Rajeev Prakash). 8 vessels delivered to Navy in FY26 (incl. Project 17A frigates and SVL ships). L1 on next-gen corvette order ~Rs 33,000 Cr — contractual negotiation stage, Q1FY27 finalization expected.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty India Defence +20% YTD in 2026 vs Nifty50 -7%. Indian shipbuilding/defence in structural multi-decade upcycle — Navy targeting 200+ ship fleet, indigenisation push. Mazagon Dock Q4 PAT Rs 674 Cr (+107% YoY) confirms sector-wide earnings strength. Defence pack rallying on May 7 on order inflows and earnings momentum.
stocks.peer_comparison: GRSE FY26 revenue Rs 7,002 Cr smaller than Mazagon Dock but growth (+29% Q4 revenue, +24% PAT) is competitive. Multibagger cohort with Mazagon (3Y +500%+) and Cochin Shipyard. Order book visibility strong via ~Rs 33,000 Cr corvette L1 status. Antique raised TP on Q4 surprise; sector-wide TP upgrades post-results.
red_flags: Stock +48% in April — extreme overbought, mean-reversion risk
Some brokerage TPs below current trading levels — limited near-term upside
Government Nominee Director change April 27 — typical PSU rotation but worth monitoring
Order conversion/timing risk on Rs 33,000 Cr corvette order (Q1FY27 expected)
Defence stocks have whipsawed earlier in 2026 — analyst views split
catalysts: Rs 33,000 Cr corvette order finalization (L1) expected in Q1FY27
Final dividend Rs 6.70/share
Continued export and indigenous order pipeline
Defence Budget allocations and Atmanirbhar Bharat push
Antique TP raised on Q4 surprise; sector-wide upgrades
